message:
fix(aws): handle multi-line athena queries + allow bare trailing ;

Two related review-pass fixes on the Athena query rules.

1. Multi-line commands (dotall). Shell allows newlines inside quoted
   strings, so `--query-string "DELETE FROM t WHERE id=1;\nDROP TABLE t"`
   is a valid single AWS CLI invocation. The `.` in the destructive
   patterns (`athena-query-drop-database`, `-drop-table`, `-truncate`,
   `-delete-without-where`) didn't match newlines by default, so a
   DROP on line 2 was invisible — the only rule that could fire was
   `athena-query-delete-without-where` (DELETE on line 1 is visible).
   The command got blocked but under a misleading reason.

   Add the `s` flag (`(?is)`) to all four so `.` matches `\n` and the
   more accurate verb-specific rule fires.

2. Bare trailing `;` false-positive. The `(?!.*;)` lookahead on
   `athena-delete-with-where` rejected a common habit from psql /
   sqlite / other SQL CLIs: ending a single statement with `;`.
   That meant `DELETE FROM t WHERE id=1;` (no compound, just trailing
   semicolon) got blocked as an unscoped DELETE.

   Tighten the lookahead to `(?!.*;\s*[A-Za-z])`: a `;` is only a
   problem when followed by another SQL verb (letter). Bare `;`,
   `;` + line comment (`; -- …`), and `;` + block comment (`; /* … */`)
   are all allowed. Real multi-statement (`; DROP`, `;\nDROP`,
   `;DELETE`) still blocks — the test for this case now also asserts
   the correct per-verb reason is reported.

Regression coverage:
- `athena_delete_trailing_semicolon_without_second_statement_is_allowed`
  — bare `;`, `;` + line comment, `;` + block comment all allowed;
  `; DROP TABLE` and `;\nDROP TABLE` still blocked.
- Existing `athena_destructive_query_with_safe_keyword_in_comment_still_blocked`
  now passes the `;\nDROP` case with reason "DROP TABLE" (not
  "DELETE without WHERE") thanks to the dotall fix.

Full suite: 2,132 passed, 0 failed.
